label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, I need to figure out which statement is true about folk medicine based on the given text. Let's start by reading the question and the options again. The text says that folk medicine uses plants and herbs from the surroundings to keep the body healthy. It mentions that materials for making medicine are everywhere, so it can be practiced anywhere. Then it goes into how early humans learned from animals, like eating snakeroot after a snake bite. Also, when animals are sick, they rest and use plants and pure air to recover. The text also says that some think folk medicine includes old wives' tales, like hanging a vegetable material around the neck for health, but those are fairy tales and not part of the actual folk medicine discussion. Now, looking at the options: A) It keeps one healthy by using medicines taken from plants and herbs. The first sentence of the text says "Folk medicine keeps the body healthy by using medicines taken from surrounding plants and herbs." So A seems to match directly. B) It can treat most kinds of diseases for both man and animals. The text doesn't say "most kinds of diseases." It talks about using plants to treat sickness, but there's no mention of it being able to treat "most" diseases for both. The part about animals is about how humans learned to treat themselves by observing animals, not that folk medicine treats animals. So B might not be correct. C) It is better than other medicines in curing serious diseases. The text doesn't compare folk medicine to other medicines in terms of effectiveness, especially for serious diseases. There's no mention of it being "better," so C is likely incorrect. D) It was a way of body treatment used only in man's early history. The text says that the use of folk medicine goes back
